title: Ayat al-Kursi Decoded: The Greatest Verse in the Quran

sound.ambient: deep calm cosmic ambience, soft airy hum, vast and reverent, subtle shimmer, no music, no melody, no instruments

_note: Surah Decoded #3 - Ayat al-Kursi (2:255, the Throne Verse). Generic data-driven Decoded kit + cosmic 'heavens' backdrop (midnight theme). Calm tone. Verse 2:255 auto-pulled from Quran.com. Protection hadith (Sahih al-Bukhari 2311) verified + on fact sheet; greatest-verse hadith (Sahih Muslim) cited in narration. No em dashes.

segments.text: There is a single verse the Prophet, peace be upon him, called the greatest in the entire Quran. Read it before you sleep, and a guardian from Allah stays with you until morning. Tonight, we decode it.
segments.caption: The greatest verse, decoded.

segments.text: Ayat al-Kursi. The Verse of the Throne. Verse two hundred and fifty-five, in the heart of Surah al-Baqarah.
The greatest single verse in the Quran. Found in its longest chapter. And recited every night, for protection.

Listen to how it opens. There is no god but Him, the Ever-Living, the Sustainer of all that exists. No tiredness touches Him. No sleep. And everything in the heavens and the earth belongs to Him alone.

data.heading: One verse, seven movements
rows: No god but Him, the Living
All the heavens and earth are His
His Throne spans the heavens and earth
The Most High, the Most Great

segments.text: In just one verse, there are seven movements. From His oneness, to His perfect life, to a Throne that spans all of creation. Watch them unfold.

data.question: It is the greatest verse because it is entirely about...

segments.text: And here is what sets it apart. It is the one verse that is entirely about a single subject. Can you guess what? Comment it.

segments.arabic: إِذَا أَوَيْتَ إِلَى فِرَاشِكَ فَاقْرَأْ آيَةَ الْكُرْسِيِّ، لَنْ يَزَالَ عَلَيْكَ مِنَ اللَّهِ حَافِظٌ، وَلَا يَقْرَبَنَّكَ شَيْطَانٌ حَتَّى تُصْبِحَ
segments.translation: When you go to your bed, recite Ayat al-Kursi. A guardian from Allah will stay with you, and no devil will come near you until morning.
segments.text: The Prophet, peace be upon him, confirmed that whoever recites it at night will have a guardian from Allah, and no devil will come near him until morning.
And when he asked his companion which verse in the Book of Allah was the greatest, the answer was this one. He smiled, and said: may knowledge be pleasant for you.

data.heading: What it teaches
items: It is the greatest verse in the Quran.
It is entirely about Allah, His names and power.
Read it at night and a guardian stays till dawn.
Read it after each prayer; Paradise awaits.

segments.text: So remember four things. It is the greatest verse. It is entirely about Allah. Read it at night for protection. And read it after every prayer, and Paradise is promised.
One verse. The Throne of the Most High, over all the heavens and the earth. Read Ayat al-Kursi tonight, before you sleep.
segments.caption: Read it tonight.

segments.text: And comment the surah or verse you want me to decode next.
segments.caption: Which one next?

segments.text: And our app, Ketabi, is a calm home for your faith. Get it free, at Ketabi Studio dot com.
segments.caption: Download Ketabi.

sources: Ayat al-Kursi - Quran 2:255, Surah al-Baqarah (the Throne Verse) - auto-pulled
Sahih al-Bukhari 2311 - recite Ayat al-Kursi at night; a guardian from Allah stays, no devil approaches until morning
Sahih Muslim 810 - the Prophet asked Ubayy ibn Ka'b for the greatest verse in the Book of Allah; he answered Ayat al-Kursi
Sunan an-Nasa'i (al-Kubra), graded sahih by al-Albani - whoever recites Ayat al-Kursi after every obligatory prayer, nothing keeps him from Paradise but death
